settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Change the settings of the application

infoGeneral.title: ENreco Archive
infoGeneral.welcome: Welcome to ENreco Archive! A fan project dedicated to archiving (almost) everything that transpired during the events of {series}.
infoGeneral.whatIsIntro: ENigmatic Recollection, or <bold>{shortName}</bold> for short, is:
infoGeneral.officialDescription: A collection of stories in which the members of hololive English play a part. Through streams, animations, and songs wrought anew, immerse yourself in fresh narratives woven from myriad realms beyond.
infoGeneral.explanation: In short, ENreco is where our favorite Hololive English talents come together, interact, and create stories through roleplay. The series is split into different chapters, each focusing on a specific Hololive English generation, with its own unique setting and lore.
infoGeneral.howToContent: For instructions on how to use the Archive and what features it provides, please refer to the Guide section.
infoGeneral.notesContent: Some summary details may be missed or not included. For the full experience, you can:
infoGeneral.checkClips: Check out compilations made by fans.
infoGeneral.teamInfo: If you have any questions regarding the project, feel free to reach out to Hiro via <twitter-link>{twitter}</twitter-link> or send an email to <mail-link>{email}.</mail-link>
infoGeneral.performanceNote: Note: If you experience major lag when using the site, try switching to a Chromium-based browser (like Chrome, Opera, or Edge).
infoGeneral.guidelinesContent: This is a non-profit fan project and is not affiliated with COVER Corp. nor Mojang. The site uses music taken from the talents' streams, as well as sound effects and assets from Minecraft, following <cover-link>{coverGuidelines}</cover-link> and <minecraft-link>{minecraftGuidelines}.</minecraft-link>
infoGeneral.feedbackNote: For any feedback or to report issues, please use this link here

infoGuide.mainDescription: These are the main features for content related things about the event. Use these features to catch up on the story, check what you've missed, or simply relive the events
infoGuide.fanworks: You can also occasionally find links to fanworks such as art, music, or animation at the end of each card.
infoGuide.readMarkers: To help you keep track of your progress, there are read markers at the end of each card. To have an overall view of which cards you have read, click the counter at the top to open the Read Status tab.
infoGuide.contentFormat: Cards use special color-coded links, clicking them would result in different actions:
infoGuide.timestamps: Opens a stream clip in a card to watch
infoGuide.linkers: Jumps directly to a referenced card
infoGuide.texts: Opens a full in-world or talent-written text
infoGuide.navigation: Use the controls at the bottom to move between days and chapters.
infoGuide.interaction: Click any character or relationship to open their card. Dimmed nodes had no activity that day. Zoom with your mouse wheel or trackpad.
infoGuide.dayRecap: Each day starts with a Day Recap summarizing its events. Reopen it anytime via the top-right icon, and toggle character/relationship visibility from there.
infoGuide.recommendedOrder: For the smoothest experience, we suggest:
infoGuide.freeExploration: But feel free to explore in any order you like!
infoGuide.glossaryDescription: Summaries of characters, storylines and more.
infoGuide.glossaryText1: The Glossary Tab, represented by the library icon on the top left, serves as a comprehensive reference for all characters, storylines, and important terms related to the event.
infoGuide.glossaryText2: It provides detailed summaries and descriptions, allowing you to quickly look up information about specific characters or storylines without having to search through the entire content.
infoGuide.chapterRecapDescription: Read the key events of each chapter
infoGuide.chapterRecapContent: The Chapter Recap Tab, which can be found on the right side with the book icon, provides concise summaries of each chapter's key events. This feature is designed to help you quickly catch up on the story or refresh your memory about specific chapters without having to reread everything.
infoGuide.otherFeaturesDescription: These features help with the overall experience of using the site. You can listen to music used in the event, checkout hundreds of fanart made by the community, watch clips, news, and more!
infoGuide.jukeboxDescription: Listen to music used in the event
infoGuide.jukeboxContent: Want to be more immersive in exploring the content? The Jukebox Tab, represented by the music disc icon, allows you to listen to the music used in the event. Whether it's background music, character themes, or iconic tracks, you can enjoy them all in one place while navigating through the content. It's a great way to enhance your experience and connect more deeply with the story and its atmosphere.
infoGuide.libestalGalleryDescription: View fanart made by the community
infoGuide.libestalGalleryContent: The most amazing thing about ENreco is the community that has formed around it. The Libestal Gallery Tab, represented by the art palette icon, is a dedicated space to showcase the incredible fanart created by the community. Here, you can browse through hundreds of fanart pieces inspired by the event, created by talented artists who share their love for the story and characters.
infoGuide.newsContent: Stay updated with the latest official news about ENreco through the News Tab, which is represented by the news icon. This section provides you with the most recent announcements, updates, and important information related to the event.
infoGuide.mediaArchiveContent1: The Media Archive Tab, represented by the video icon on the top left, is a treasure trove of both fan made clips as well as official trailers, and other media related to the event.
infoGuide.mediaArchiveContent2: Not only that, you can also read every single written book that was found in the event, from journal entries made by the talents to official lore books. And, more importantly, you can also read fanfics written by the community!
infoGuide.bingoDescription: Make your own ENreco-themed bingo card
infoGuide.bingoContent1: So many unexpected things happen in this story, so why not turn it into a game? You can now create your own ENreco-themed bingo card in the Bingo tab.
infoGuide.bingoContent2: Write your predictions, mark off events as they happen, make a new card each day, or use the preset options we've prepared. You can also export and share your card with friends or on social media.
infoGuide.settingsContent1: Last but not least, the Settings Tab, represented by the gear icon, allows you to customize your experience and preferences on the site.

infoCredits.title: Credits
infoCredits.specialThanksDiscord: To everyone at the ENigmatic Recollection Lore Discord Server for providing additional resources and emotional support, as well as to the artists featured in the Fanart Gallery for granting us permission to use their work in this project. All artworks and their creators are properly credited and can be found in that section.
infoCredits.specialThanksLyger: To <link>lyger</link>, the official scenario writer for ENigmatic Recollection, for assisting with the proofreading of all lore-related content and offering invaluable feedback for the site.

gambling.label: Color Cannon (Chapter 1)
gambling.intro: The most frequently visited minigame—or rather, venue—of Chapter One was undoubtedly the <bold>Amber Coin Casino</bold> <icon></icon>, where everyone could indulge in their favorite pastime: gambling.
gambling.rules: The casino's signature minigame functions like a roulette of sorts. A board displays four different colors, each occupying a set number of squares with a corresponding multiplier:
gambling.gameplay: Players can choose how much to bet on each color. Once the game begins, colors are eliminated one by one, slowly whittling down the board—until only one remained.
gambling.winning: Whoever bets on the final color would receive their wager multiplied accordingly.

memory.label: Mind's Eye (Chapter 1)
memory.intro: <bold>The Scarlet Wand <icon></icon>'s Memory Game</bold> was introduced on Day 7 of the journey. As its name suggests, its main purpose is to test the memory of our heroes.
memory.gameplay: Upon starting the game, each round presents a white board where a number of squares light up in one of four colors—red, green, blue, or yellow. After about two seconds, the colors vanish, and the player must recreate the original pattern.
memory.scoring: If they get it right, their score increases, and the difficulty ramps up, presenting a more complex pattern. If they get it wrong, they lose points, and the difficulty decreases.
memory.multiplayer: The game continues until the 60-second timer runs out. Up to four players can compete at the same time, and whoever has the highest score at the end earns bragging rights.

chicken.label: Chicken Rescue (Chapter 1)
chicken.intro: Just like the Scarlet Wand, the Cerulean Cup <icon></icon> launched their own minigame on Day 7. As representatives of empathy, they designed a game that would help spread that very value: <bold>Catching Chickens</bold>.
chicken.gameplay: As the name suggests, the goal is simple—catch chickens… but empathetically. Players are presented with a board where chickens continuously spawn from the top and slowly fall downward. The objective? Move your basket horizontally along the bottom and catch as many falling chickens as possible within the time limit.

shiori.label: Commission Shiori (Chapter 2)
shiori.intro: Throughout the second chapter, true to her role as the Archiver, <bold>Shiori</bold> had written a variety of fanfics and memory pieces as commissions. These ranged from romantic and questionable tales, to NSFW content, as well as more serious reflections on past comrades.
shiori.options: If you're curious about exploring Shiori's collection, which includes books written by her as well as others, you have two options: follow her journey (as well as others') day by day, or correctly answer 10 questions here to unlock her full archive.
shiori.challenge: Let's see how well you really know Shiorin.
shiori.question: Question

bingo.label: Bingo Maker (Chapter 3)
bingo.desc: Make your predictions of what will happen, and generate an ENreco-themed Bingo Board!
bingo.intro: Create your own <bold>ENigmatic Recollection Bingo</bold> sheet for Chapter 3: Broken Bonds! Fill in the squares with predictions, moments you want to see, or inside jokes from the community.
bingo.editModeDesc: <bold>Edit Mode:</bold> Click on any square to edit its text. Click outside to save.
bingo.markModeDesc: <bold>Mark Mode:</bold> Click on squares to mark them as complete
bingo.downloadDesc: <bold>Download:</bold> Save your bingo sheet as an image to share with friends!
bingo.presetDesc: <bold>Presets:</bold> Stuck on what to put? Use community-made presets for inspiration or a quick setup!
bingo.shareDesc: <bold>Sharing your Board:</bold> You can let others get a copy of your board via the Share and Import functions
bingo.tips: Your bingo board is automatically saved, so you can come back anytime!
bingo.cardByDay: <bold>Card by Day:</bold> You are encouraged to fill out the bingo sheet based on the events of each day, but feel free to fill it out however you like.
bingo.previewDescription: Preview how your bingo will look when downloaded
bingo.previewNote: Note: The downloaded image will fix issues with font size and text overflow.
bingo.shareDescription: Let others use a copy of your bingo board!
bingo.shareNote: Copy the code to share your board. Recipients can paste the code in the Import window to load it directly.
bingo.importDescription: Paste a share code to load someone else's bingo board
bingo.importNote: This will replace the current board with the imported one. Any marked squares will be cleared.
bingo.importErrorInvalid: Invalid share code. Please check and try again.
bingo.fullBoardMessage: Board is full. There must be empty squares to use the preset feature.
bingo.resetMessage: This will clear the current board. Are you sure?

showAll.label: Any
showAll.description: Show artworks that have any of the selected characters

hasAny.label: All
hasAny.description: Show artworks that include all the selected characters in that artwork

hasOnly.label: Only
hasOnly.description: Show artworks that include only the selected characters and no one else

lightbox.relatedTo: Related to events on Day {day}, Chapter {chapter}

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.description: Here are the latest changes to the Archive
changelog.forQuestions: For questions or to report issues, contact <link>@hiroavrs</link>

stats.title: Daily Records
stats.noContinuousChoices: There were no continuous choices on this day
stats.noOneTimeChoices: There were no one-time choices on this day

info.text1: This is where you can view the decisions and answers made by the heroes to the questions they are asked each day throughout Chapter 3, along with the teams they belong to
info.text2: You can also track how their choices change over time, as the heroes may change their minds on certain matters as the days progress

spoilers.title: Thou Shall Not Pass!
spoilers.description: Just a heads-up—this page will not be updated until after chapter three! This page also contains spoilers, if you haven’t experienced the story of ENigmatic Recollection yet, you might want to check it out first.
spoilers.agree: *By continuing you agree to not hold the ENreco Archive responsible for being spoiled.

weapons.description: This category contains information about the weapons, known as Revelations, used by the heroes — including their abilities, appearances, and other related details.
weapons.credit: All entry thumbnails and models in this category are by <link>Soleiss.</link>

characters.description: This category contains information about characters, their personalities, and their stories in each chapter.
characters.credit: All entry thumbnails in this category are by <link>DDOLBANG.</link>

lore.description: This category contains information about the story of ENigmatic Recollection, including both official in-universe lore and self-made stories created by the heroes.
lore.note: <bold>Note:</bold> Regarding the heroes' stories, only consistent and prevalent ones are included in this category.

quests.description: This category contains information about the journey's quests, specifically the dungeons and missions required to advance the story.

miscellaneous.description: This category contains information about miscellaneous topics in the ENreco universe, including its mechanics, special events, and other related details.

videoArchive.title: Media Archive
videoArchive.description: A collection of official media, streams and memories

clipArchive.title: Clip Archive
clipArchive.description: A collection of notable clips from the ENreco streams

textArchive.title: Text Archive
textArchive.description: A collection of notable writings from ENreco
textArchive.fanficError: Failed to load story content, try again later or you may read it from the original source <link>here</link>.

countdownCard.updateShouldBeOut: Update should now be out (Ctrl + R) — if not, please be patient 🙏

comingSoonCard.text1: <bold>Chapter 3: Broken Bonds</bold> updates will be here as the event happens!
comingSoonCard.text2: To be up to date, you can check out more news and announcements <link>here.</link>
comingSoonCard.note: Note that updates may not always be exactly at the scheduled time due to various factors, but we will do our best to keep them consistent!
